title: consolidation gap: re-add import-linter as toggle + contracts LOCAL-SLOT (all 3 repos use it)
description: Audit of cardiac/mindscape/synth: ALL THREE enforce import-linter (>=1 forbidden contract, min 'core is an independent kernel'). Scaffold dropped it in the de-opinionate refactor arguing contracts are project-specific + graph.py covers cycles. But graph.py _cycles catches only bidirectional cycles, NOT one-way forbidden imports (core->trainer no-cycle passes graph, fails import-linter). The GATE is universal; only the CONTRACTS vary. Fix: enable_import_linter bool (default true); ships [tool.importlinter] with root_packages=packages + a kernel-independence starter contract (packages[0] forbids importing packages[1:]) in a LOCAL-SLOT, ONLY when >1 package (single-package = no layers). Wire lint-imports into nox/ci/pre-commit under the same guard. Render assertion + a bite-test (inject upward import -> fails).

title: de-opinionate template layout: drop core/viewer layers, single package, arch-fitness layer-agnostic
description: Scaffold was shipping the 3-repo core->trainer->viewer layering as imposed structure. Guardrails must be layout-agnostic. Drop core/ + viewer layer + has_viewer/viewer_name/viewer_imports_trainer toggles. Ship ONE package {{ package_name }} (two internal modules: math_ops<-pipeline, keeps an import edge + astgrep-compliant). Remove import-linter from defaults (directional contracts are project-specific; graph.py covers cycles) -> document as opt-in. graph.py --assert stays as the layer-agnostic arch gate (god/cycle/file-size). Update pyproject/noxfile/ci/pre-commit/conftest/test_e2e/copier.yml/docs. Re-green E2E.

title: E501 is COSMETIC -> make it advisory in the base (not enforced) — contradicts the bug-finder-not-cosmetics rule
description: From cardiac-seg's v0.17 runner adoption (bd cardiac-seg-44o4). The base enforced-lint select includes E501 (line-too-long, line-length 120). But E501 is a COSMETIC rule (line length), which contradicts the scaffold's own stated philosophy: gates are BUG-FINDERS, not cosmetics (SPEC + copier.yml deliberately excludes UP/C4 as 'opinionated churn'). E501 is exactly that kind of style opinion. IMPACT: cardiac-seg deliberately keeps E501 ADVISORY (bd cardiac-seg-i851 — a dense hand-tuned style, 138 lines run >120 on purpose for readability). Adopting the template runner as-is enforces E501 -> 138 red on cardiac's own shipped code, blocking the full runner adoption (44o4). FIX (owner-chosen 2026-07-14): move E501 from the ENFORCED select to the ADVISORY run (the 'ruff check . --statistics' step + pyproject can still surface it), so line-length is a signal not a merge gate — for ALL 3 repos, not a per-repo carve-out (so it's not a dodge: E501 is advisory house-wide). A repo that WANTS hard line-length can opt in, but the house default is advisory. VERIFY: an ml gen + cardiac both pass enforced lint with long lines; E501 still shows in the advisory --statistics output. Unblocks cardiac-seg-44o4 (the rest of the v0.17 runner is verified no-loss: F722/S101/CARDIAC_DATA/wide-scope/ci-slots all work; E501 is the sole blocker). Origin: cardiac-seg-44o4 / i851.
close_reason: DONE v0.18.0. E501 moved from the ENFORCED union select to the advisory '--statistics --extend-select E501,SLF001' surface (ci + nox) — reported, never a merge gate. House-wide (owner-chosen), not a per-repo carve-out. Unblocks cardiac-seg-44o4: its 138 intentional >120 lines no longer red-CI on the base runner. e2e asserts E501 absent from enforced select + present on the advisory surface. 92 tests pass.

title: SLF001 in the union conflicts with the mandated op-namespace pattern — needs a ruff select/ignore local-slot
description:
v0.14 added SLF001 (private-member-access) to the maximal union select. It STRUCTURALLY conflicts with the py-top-level-function ast-grep gate, which mandates every helper live on a class as a (usually static) method. Consequence: a stateless sibling-helper call OR a module-level function registry must reference `Cls._helper` — which SLF001 flags, though it is NOT a cross-boundary reach-in (ruff cannot express 'same module/class'). Any consumer that did the free-func -> static-method migration hits this en masse: mindscape (269 funcs migrated) got 99 SLF001 hits across 25 files on the v0.13->v0.16 update — registries (FNIRS_FEATURE_FNS = {name: DescriptorBank._f_mean, ...}), cross-sibling static calls (Amplitude._time_axis from DescriptorBank), and runner main-helpers.

The two rules are both scaffold-owned, so the scaffold must reconcile them. Options: (a) drop SLF001 from the union; (b) add a repo-level ruff select/ignore LOCAL-SLOT (parallel to the pep8-naming ignore-names and vulture-ignore-names FACT slots) so a consumer can exempt SLF001 without hand-editing the template-owned [tool.ruff.lint] block — which the consumer policy forbids ('don't hand-edit template-owned to pass a gate'); (c) narrow SLF001's intent to genuine cross-class reach-ins (not expressible in ruff today). 

Recommend (b): the set of union rules that conflict with a given repo's structure is a per-repo FACT, exactly like the naming/dead-code vocab slots — so a select-subtract / extra-ignore slot is the consistent mechanism. mindscape currently carves SLF001 via the per-file-ignores LOCAL-SLOT (runner tree + static-op-namespace/registry core files) as a stopgap; a proper slot lets it drop the file-list and just subtract SLF001 with a documented reason.
close_reason: DONE v0.18.0. SLF001 resolved via the SAME mechanism as E501 (4c2): demoted from the enforced union to the advisory --extend-select surface, house-wide. Rationale: SLF001 STRUCTURALLY conflicts with the py-top-level-function ast-grep gate (that gate forces same-module Cls._helper references SLF001 mis-reads as reach-ins; ruff can't express 'same module'; 99 FP on mindscape). Chose the advisory-demote over the recommended select-subtract LOCAL-SLOT (option b) because a per-repo rule slot = the o70 dodge vector the owner rejected; the conflict is universal (every migrated repo hits it), so the fix belongs in the base for all 3, not a slot. mindscape can now DROP its per-file-ignores SLF001 stopgap. e2e asserts SLF001 absent from enforced select.

title: Runner can't yet express an ml/consumer rule superset — 3 gaps from cardiac 44o4 (F722-ignore BUG, engine argv default, ci repo-step slots)
description:
Found during cardiac-seg's v0.15 adoption (bd cardiac-seg-44o4). The generated runner can't carry a consumer's rule superset without loss. 3 gaps, #1 is a BUG affecting every fresh ml generation:

## GAP 1 (BUG): enforced-lint CLI lacks --ignore F722 for domain=ml
The rendered ci.yml enforced-lint is 'uvx ruff check <lint_paths> --select F,B,...'. An explicit --select BYPASSES pyproject [tool.ruff.lint] ignore, so F722 (in the F family) FIRES on jaxtyping shape strings (Float[Tensor bchw]) — which the template itself ships for domain=ml. Measured on cardiac: 164 F722 errors under the template's exact enforced-lint line. So a FRESH ml repo that writes any jaxtyping annotation gets a red CI from its own generated config. FIX: the enforced-lint CLI adds --ignore F722 when enable_ml (mirror pyproject's ignore on the CLI), OR drive lint from pyproject config instead of an explicit --select. Verify: an ml gen with a jaxtyping boundary passes its own enforced lint.

## GAP 2 (papercut): base engine argv defaults to 'src', not the packages list
devtools/*.py (graph, state_candidates, lcom, data_clumps, magic_literals, shape_contracts) default their positional packages arg to ['src']. The rendered runner passes packages explicitly so it is fine — but any hand-written/no-arg invocation scans a nonexistent 'src' and silently checks nothing (e.g. shape_contracts --assert with no packages = 0 boundaries = vacuous PASS). cardiac hit this converging engines while keeping its own ci (had to add 'core cardioseg' to every call). FIX: render each engine's argparse default to the copier packages list, or make no-arg a hard error not a silent 'src'.

## GAP 3 (design): no ci repo-step LOCAL-SLOTs for repo-specific gates
The template ci has no slot for consumer-specific steps, so a repo whose CI is a superset of the base can't adopt the runner without losing steps. cardiac's superset the template ci can't express: (a) data-skip env var NAME — cardiac reads CARDIAC_DATA, the template renders CARDIOSEG_DATA (project-name-derived) so tests would error not skip; the env var name is a per-repo FACT, needs a slot/answer; (b) pytest --ignore=<viewer tests> + a SEPARATE viewer coverage step (keeps the floor calibrated to library logic); (c) an extra advisory step (PLR2004 un-silenced); (d) shape_contracts --assert BLOCKING while base ships it advisory (per-repo graduation — cardiac is boundary-clean so it blocks; base graduates all-ml-clean later). FIX: ci repo-step LOCAL-SLOT region(s) + a data-env-var answer, so a consumer's superset rides in slots (mechanism identical, contents per-repo) instead of forcing a fork or a loss.

Until these land, cardiac keeps its own ci.yml (rule superset preserved) running the base engines via explicit 'core cardioseg' calls; 44o4 (full runner adoption) completes when the template can express the superset. Origin: cardiac-seg-44o4 + the v0.15 adoption pass.
close_reason: Fixed all 3 gaps in the template (v0.17.0). GAP1 (BUG): enforced-lint CLI now passes --ignore F722 when enable_ml in ci.yml/noxfile/pre-commit — an explicit --select bypasses pyproject ignore, so a fresh ml gen with a jaxtyping annotation no longer red-CIs on its own config. Proven: ruff --select F fires 2xF722 on Float[Tensor,'b c h w'] dim strings, --ignore F722 clears them. GAP2: every devtools engine (graph/shape_contracts/data_clumps/lcom/magic_literals/state_candidates) is now nargs='+' — a no-arg call is an argparse error not a silent 'src' scan (which made shape_contracts --assert vacuously PASS). GAP3: added data_env_var (when:false, project-derived default, overridable — the ml data-skip env NAME is a per-repo FACT) + two ci LOCAL-SLOT regions (ci-lint-steps/ci-test-steps) so a consumer CI superset rides on slots not a fork. Tests: 92 passed. Unblocks cardiac-seg-44o4 (full runner adoption — the template can now express cardiac's ci superset without loss).

title: Add LICENSE — public template repo has none (blocks reuse)

title: Investigation: hygiene-scope widen (ruff/jscpd over viewer+tests+web-TS) — reject vip.3 conform-drop under no-dodge
description: Gap from cardiac-seg adoption (xwji audit). Parent: vip epic. vip.3 fixed ruff+jscpd = packages (core cardioseg), 'conform don't fork'. But cardiac lints 'core cardioseg cardioview tests' and jscpd's 'core cardioseg cardioview/web/src' (TS). CONFORMING = DROPPING lint coverage on tests + the viewer + TS duplication-detection = a REAL coverage LOSS. Owner rule (no-dodge / superset-if-no-conflict): a repo must NOT lose functionality to adopt. So the base must EXPRESS the wider hygiene scope — the 'hygiene-adds' idea vip.3 floated (R1 hygiene ruff/vulture can go wider than the R2/R3 arch set) but did NOT implement. FIX: a hygiene-scope-widen LOCAL-SLOT / computed var (lint_packages|default(packages), jscpd_paths|default(packages)) so a repo with a viewer/tests-worth-linting keeps them in ruff+jscpd; single-package repos default to packages. Pure superset, NO conflict — this REOPENS the vip.3 conform-decision for the hygiene axis specifically (vip.3's own R1>R2/R3 principle supports it). Origin: cardiac-seg-xwji.
close_reason: Done v0.15.0: R1 hygiene gates (ruff+jscpd) can scan WIDER than the R2/R3 arch set. Added lint_paths + jscpd_paths computed vars (when:false, default = space-joined packages). ruff enforced + jscpd now render from them across nox (LINT_LAYERS/JSCPD_LAYERS)/CI/pre-commit; graph.py + ast-grep stay on the package arch set. A repo widens by setting lint_paths/jscpd_paths in .copier-answers.yml (e.g. cardiac: 'core cardioseg cardioview tests' + 'core cardioseg cardioview/web/src'), so it keeps viewer+tests+web-TS coverage on adoption — no functionality lost to conform (owner no-dodge rule; reopens vip.3's conform-drop for the hygiene axis per its own R1>R2/R3 principle). Pure superset, default unchanged for single-package repos. e2e: widen render test.

title: Absorb analytics.py (generic code-health explorer) into the template — it isn't mindscape-specific
description:
Raised by owner during mindscape v0.10 adoption. mindscape carries devtools/analytics.py, which the consumer-devtools-test-deletion policy (0lh) treated as a legit consumer-OWN exception. On inspection it is NOT genuinely consumer-specific — it is a GENERIC code-analytics explorer that was simply written in mindscape and never upstreamed. Absorb it into the template.

## What it is (mindscape devtools/analytics.py, bd 70b there)
A one-shot EXPLORER (like devtools.graph's report mode / magic_literals), NOT a CI gate:
`python -m devtools.analytics [--areas ...] [--top-n N] [--flag-over 250]`. Per-area + per-file:
- code lines (non-blank, non-comment, pure-ast, no radon dep)
- def count
- branch-node complexity proxy (McCabe decision points: If/For/While/Try/Except/With/IfExp/comprehension/BoolOp)
- branches-per-def (the number to watch — logic leaking into fat leaves)
- src-vs-test line ratio
- top-N largest files, + optional --flag-over budget list
Pure `ast` + `pathlib` + `dataclass`. ZERO domain logic (no ML/EEG/mlflow). Reads like a sibling of the shipped detectors.

## Why it belongs in the scaffold
- It is generic code-health tooling every house repo benefits from (complexity-per-def drift, src:test ratio, biggest-file radar). Same category as the graph explorer.
- The ONLY repo-flavored line is the default `_AREAS = ["core","neuroscan","baselines","devtools"]`. Generalize exactly like the other tools generalize scope: default areas = the `packages` answer + `devtools` (the LAYERS pattern), overridable via `--areas`.
- Keeping it consumer-local means each repo that wants it re-copies it (the very duplication 0lh removes for the other detectors).

## Proposal
1. Add `template/devtools/analytics.py` (generalize `_AREAS` from `packages`), advisory explorer — NOT wired as a blocking gate (matches its one-shot nature; optionally add an advisory nox/CI line like the class-shape explorers, or leave it explorer-only).
2. Add its tests to the scaffold's `tests/unit/test_devtools.py` (the analytics unit tests currently in mindscape's `tests/unit/devtools/test_analytics.py` move HERE — mindscape KEPT that test under 0lh precisely because analytics was consumer-owned; absorbing flips it to template-owned).
3. On the next tag, mindscape (+ siblings) `copier update`: analytics.py becomes template-owned, mindscape DELETES its local `devtools/analytics.py` + `tests/unit/devtools/test_analytics.py` (now the scaffold's), converging fully — after which mindscape has ZERO consumer-local devtools.

## Answer to "is it specific to mindscape"
No. It's generic; it was just born in mindscape. This is the mirror image of 76i (mindscape's detector EXCLUSIONS the scaffold should absorb) — a mindscape devtool the scaffold should absorb. Both flow the same direction: consumer-grown tooling -> house bar.

Ref: mindscape-588n (adoption), sdlc-scaffold-0lh (consumer devtools-test policy — analytics is its open exception), 76i (sibling upstream-from-mindscape).
close_reason: Absorbed mindscape's analytics.py into template/devtools (generalized _AREAS->['src'], explorer-only, not gate-wired) + its 2 unit tests into tests/unit/test_devtools.py + devtools/README + e2e ships-check. 72 passed. Shipped v0.13.0. Mirror of 76i (consumer-grown tool -> house bar). Consumers get it via copier update, then delete their local copy (mindscape-a6nm updated). Unblocks 0lh (no legit consumer-local devtools remain).

title: Devtools tests belong ONLY in the scaffold — delete tests/unit/devtools from consumer repos
description:
Raised by owner during mindscape v0.10 adoption (bd mindscape-588n). ARGUE + decide: consumer repos should NOT carry devtools detector tests — the scaffold is the single home.

## The argument
1. `devtools/*.py` (graph, lcom, data_clumps, state_candidates, magic_literals, omit) is TEMPLATE-OWNED — shipped byte-identical to every consumer (verified: all 16 template-owned files in mindscape are byte-identical to a fresh v0.10 generation). It is the scaffold's code living in the consumer, not the consumer's code.
2. The scaffold ALREADY tests it: `tests/unit/test_devtools.py` = 30 tests / 358 lines covering lcom (+interface/abstract skips), data_clumps, state_candidates (+pydantic/command skips), structure-cfg, god-module/cycle/oversized/undersized, unmirrored (mirror/flat/off/omit-exempt), matches_omit, coverage_omit, magic_literals (+ratchet). The canonical home exists and is well-populated.
3. Consumer devtools tests are therefore DUPLICATE coverage of identical code, tested against the SAME source. Same code + same test = redundant by definition.
4. They contribute NOTHING to consumer gates: devtools is not in the consumer's `[tool.coverage] source` (so not in the coverage floor) and not in the graph.py `--assert` test-mirror scan (LAYERS = the shipped packages, not devtools). Deleting them changes zero consumer gate outcomes.
5. They CREATE UPDATE FRICTION — the concrete harm. On the mindscape v0.10 adoption, 4 consumer devtools tests broke because they pinned the OLD detector behavior (lcom sklearn-contract exclusion; state_candidates autograd + omit-shell skips — the exclusions v0.10 refactored away, tracked in sdlc-scaffold-76i). A consumer that tests template-owned code re-litigates every template change locally. That is exactly the coupling copier-adoption is meant to remove: the consumer trusts the template is tested UPSTREAM.

## Proposal
- POLICY: consumer repos (mindscape / cardiac-seg / synthscape) delete `tests/unit/devtools/*`. The scaffold's `tests/unit/test_devtools.py` is the sole home for detector tests.
- PRECONDITION (no net coverage loss): before/with each deletion, confirm the scaffold's test_devtools.py covers the UNION of what the consumers' devtools tests covered. Today it covers nearly all of it; the known gaps are exactly the 76i exclusions (sklearn-contract / autograd / omit-shell) — land those tests HERE as part of 76i, then the consumer tests are strictly redundant.
- MECHANISM: document it as a house convention (consumers do not test template-owned code) — e.g. in the scaffold README / agent_body partial — and, if wanted, a check that flags a `tests/unit/devtools/` dir in a consumer as drift.

## Counter to consider
The only thing a consumer devtools test could legitimately cover is consumer-SPECIFIC detector CONFIG (e.g. that ITS pyproject omit list exempts ITS shells). But that is config, testable via the detector's public behavior in the scaffold with a fixture — not a per-repo copy of the detector's unit tests. So no genuine consumer-specific coverage is lost.

Ref: mindscape-588n (adoption that surfaced this), sdlc-scaffold-76i (the exclusion tests that must land here first).
notes: mindscape EXECUTED the consumer-side deletion 2026-07-14 (bd mindscape-588n branch, PR #51): removed tests/unit/devtools/{test_lcom,test_state_candidates,test_data_clumps,test_graph,test_omit}.py. KEPT test_analytics.py — devtools/analytics.py is mindscapes OWN devtool, NOT shipped by the template (the legit exception: a consumer tests ITS OWN devtools, not the template-owned ones). The 76i precondition was MOOT for mindscape: the 4 exclusion tests (sklearn/autograd/omit-shell) were already removed during the 588n adoption, so the 5 deleted files were pure duplication the scaffolds test_devtools.py (30 tests) already covers — verified suite green (301 passed) + coverage unchanged (92%, devtools not in source). REMAINING under this policy: cardiac-seg + synthscape consumer deletions, + the house-convention doc (consumers dont test template-owned code; a consumer-only devtool like analytics.py keeps its local test) + optional drift check flagging a tests/unit/devtools/ dir that mirrors template detectors.
close_reason: DONE (xsc unblocked it). Scaffold side: policy documented in agent_body ('dont test template-owned code'); precondition met (76i landed exclusion tests, xsc absorbed analytics = the last consumer-own exception). Consumer deletions dispatched as beads per owner instruction (not deleted from here): mindscape-a6nm, cardiac-seg-7vxn (post-zjaf), synthscape-b0m (post-2zy). Optional drift-check left unbuilt (not needed).

title: Audit (cardiac-seg / origin): owner sets convergence = UNION superset, not majority — RULE vs FACT split, no rule-dodging
description:
Cross-repo audit input from cardiac-seg (the most-mature / origin repo in the vip convergence). Mirror of j08 (mindscape's adoption audit) but from the ceiling side, plus an OWNER DECISION that tightens the epic's staging. Filed for the scaffold agent to interpret; owner will ask for that interpretation.

## Owner decision (the load-bearing change)
The end-state guardrail set = the UNION superset of all 3 repos' rules — NOT the 2/3 majority the vip epic currently stages by. Verbatim intent: 'i want the guardrails to be the superset of all repos — so no dodging ruff codes and namings'. Consequence: every rule ANY repo enforces becomes house bar; every other repo fixes up to it the hard way (k8c NO-SHORTCUTS: fix-all-first, no suppression, no threshold-drop, advisory only as a staging step for the FIX not a dodge of the RULE). This cuts both ways — cardiac-seg is not exempt: it must also adopt whatever a sibling is stricter on (per j08, mindscape enforces W,E7,S101,T20 that cardiac lacks -> cardiac adopts those + fixes its hits).

## The distinction that keeps 'superset' from becoming 'fork' or 'dodge'
- RULES (ruff select codes, the gate inventory, naming/pep8-N) -> UNION base, byte-identical across all 3. No per-repo rule slots.
- FACTS (deps, scan paths, pep8 ignore-NAMES vocab, vulture domain FPs, arch thresholds) -> additive-local slots. Not dodges: monai != mne is a fact, not a loosened rule.
Explicitly REJECTED during this audit: a 'ruff-select-add' slot (base_select + local_extra). It reads additive but it is a dodge vector — it lets a repo keep NOT running a rule a sibling runs (e.g. mindscape keeping N off despite 383 hits). Codes belong in the union base; only vocab/paths/deps are local. (My earlier suggestion to add such a slot is retracted here for the record.)

## What this flips in the vip epic
- Tier 3 (vip.2, currently 'AMBIGUOUS placement, parked pending 3-repo consensus' — the rest of cardiac's ratchet: N, SLF001, PERF, S603/607, PTH123, C4/UP, pep8-naming) flips from parked -> IN THE BASE, everyone fixes. mindscape's explicit N-rejection is overruled by the union rule, not negotiated.
- Tier 2 (vip.1, ml-typing bundle jaxtyping+beartype+shape_contracts) stays domain=ml-gated, but under union it is the house bar for ALL ml-domain repos, not cardiac-local. shape_contracts should become a BASE ml-gate (not a repo-local extra gate).
- Tier 1 (7g0, magic_literals + SIM) already upstreamed — consistent, no change.

## cardiac-seg adoption facts (for when cardiac registers, mirror of mindscape-588n)
- No .copier-answers.yml (guardrails hand-built; cardiac is where magic_literals+shape_contracts were BORN and promoted up).
- Per-gate package scope, already covered by vip.3: vulture paths + import-linter roots include cardioview; coverage source + ruff lint = core,cardioseg (cardioview OUT). Confirms vip.3's 'two of three repos scope per-gate' — cardiac is the second witness.
- Non-slotted permanent-local FACTS: ~20 ML deps (torch+cu130 pin, monai, mlflow...), [tool.ruff.lint.per-file-ignores]. Same shape as mindscape step 5.
- cardiac's RULE extras that must land in the union base (not local): the ~20 ruff codes above + pep8-naming + the jaxtyping/beartype/shape_contracts ml-gate.

## Open mechanism questions for the scaffold agent
1. Union base means a new rule can't be blocking everywhere at once until every repo passes it. Confirm the staging: rule enters the base immediately (identical select list), each repo runs it ADVISORY until its hits reach zero, then graduates to blocking per-repo. That stages the fix, not the rule. Is that the model you want, or do you gate the base-select bump on all-3-clean?
2. shape_contracts -> base ml-gate: it needs a domain==ml condition (array/tensor boundary tool). Does the existing enable_ml gating cover a NEW base gate cleanly, or does adding a gate to the base need a mechanism (the 'local-gate slot' idea is now moot for cardiac since shape_contracts goes to base — but a general repo MIGHT still have a genuinely-local gate; decide if that case exists or if union forbids it too).
3. pep8-naming: cardiac runs N with a per-repo ignore-NAMES vocab (its ML/physics symbols). Under RULE-vs-FACT: N (the rule) -> base; the ignore-names vocab (the fact) -> a naming-vocab slot. Confirm a naming ignore-names slot exists or file it.

No action requested here beyond interpretation — owner drives sequencing. This bead records the origin-repo audit + the union decision so the epic's majority framing gets reconciled to it.
close_reason: Interpreted + reconciled. Q1 (staging): BLOCKING-FIRST, not advisory-until-clean — the owners own qqjs/k8c rule already rejects advisory-until-clean as procrastination. A code enters the union base and each repo fixes up on pull (red drives the migration). NO per-repo advisory-select (that IS the dodge vector). Stage per-code in TIME (rollout order), never per-repo in the rule list; reject 'gate bump on all-3-clean' (majority-gating holds the rule hostage to the slowest repo = de-facto dodge). Q2 (shape_contracts->base ml-gate): enable_ml already gates numpy/gitignore/doc-layers/data-CI, so a new {% if enable_ml %} base gate needs NO new mechanism; union forbids permanent repo-local gates, so no local-gate slot — any new gate goes to base (ml ones ride enable_ml). Q3 (pep8-naming): N=rule->base; the ignore-NAMES vocab=fact-> a NEW [tool.ruff.lint.pep8-naming] ignore-names LOCAL-SLOT (does not exist yet; folded into vip.2). RECONCILED: SPEC design-model now carries the RULE-vs-FACT union law + SIM reframed union-not-majority (committed); vip epic + vip.1 (shape_contracts=base ml-gate) + vip.2 (parked->union base, N-rejection overruled, +naming-vocab slot) updated; cardiac adoption facts -> cardiac-seg-zjaf (mirror of mindscape-588n). Owner drives per-code sequencing. Nothing shipped needed reverting (7g0/vip.3 already consistent).

title: Upstream 3 detector exclusions v0.10 dropped (sklearn-contract / autograd.Function / omit-shell)
description:
Surfaced during mindscape's v0.10 adoption (bd mindscape-588n). mindscape's hand-built advisory detectors carried THREE exclusions the scaffold's v0.10 versions lack. On adoption mindscape took the template detectors and REMOVED the tests pinning these (they now live only here, to return via `copier update` once shipped):

## 1. lcom.py — sklearn fit/transform contract exclusion
v0.10 lcom excludes disjoint-state classes by SUBCLASS (interface-impl of a domain base, or ABC/abstract). It does NOT exempt the duck-typed sklearn contract: a bare `class Scaler: def fit(self,X): self.m=...; def transform(self,X): ...` has LCOM4=2 (disjoint) and is flagged, though it's the sklearn interface, not a fused class. mindscape's detector had a NAME-BASED heuristic: `fit` + (`transform` | `__call__`) methods -> exempt. Without it, every sklearn-style transformer in a consumer repo is advisory-flagged (noise).

## 2. state_candidates.py — torch.autograd.Function skip
A `class GradReverse(Function): forward/backward` threads `ctx` by the framework API — a contract, not promotable instance state. mindscape skipped autograd.Function subclasses (like the pydantic-config skip). v0.10 skips CLI-dispatcher (add_args+run) + `__init__` classes but not autograd.Function -> flags every custom autograd op.

## 3. state_candidates.py — coverage-omit shell skip
mindscape's state_candidates read `[tool.coverage] omit` (via devtools/omit.py, shared with the test-mirror rule so the gates agree on what a shell is): a runner/adapter shell's shared params are its data, not object identity, so an omitted file isn't flagged. v0.10 state_candidates no longer reads omit.py -> flags omit-shell runners.

## Why it matters
All three are ADVISORY (never block), so this is advisory-NOISE quality, not a gate break. But the detectors are a "regression radar" — noise erodes their signal. A repo with sklearn transformers + custom autograd ops + runner shells (mindscape has all three) gets a noisier radar after adopting v0.10.

## Proposed
Upstream the three exclusions into the template detectors:
- lcom: re-add the fit/transform + fit/__call__ name-based contract exemption.
- state_candidates: re-add the autograd.Function subclass skip + the devtools/omit.py coverage-omit-shell skip.
Ship each with its test (the four removed from mindscape: test_lcom::test_transformer_contract_excluded, test_fit_call_contract_excluded; test_state_candidates::test_autograd_function_is_skipped, test_scan_skips_coverage_omit_shells). Then consumers get them back via `copier update`. Ref: mindscape-588n; the removed-test bodies are in mindscape git history (the adoption commit).
close_reason: Restored the 3 dropped exclusions into the template detectors + 4 unit tests (the ones mindscape removed on adoption): lcom sklearn fit/transform-|-__call__ contract exemption; state_candidates torch.autograd.Function skip + coverage-omit-shell skip (reads [tool.coverage] omit via omit.py). All advisory. 70 passed. Shipped v0.11.0 -> consumers get them back via copier update. Unblocks 0lh.

title: Per-gate package scoping — LOCAL-SLOT overrides defaulting to packages
description:
From j08 (mindscape v0.6 adoption audit). VERDICT: the flat single `packages` answer cant express that gates have different natural scopes — and this is GENERAL, not a mindscape quirk. Confirmed against cardioseg: vulture paths=[core,cardioseg,cardioview] + import-linter roots include cardioview, but coverage source=[core,cardioseg] + ruff lint = core cardioseg — cardioview is IN some gates, OUT of others. Two of three house repos scope per-gate. The flat list is the templates oversimplification, not the repos being wrong.

PRINCIPLE (maps to the R1/R2/R3 radius): architecture gates (graph/ast-grep/jscpd, R2-R3) scan the OWNED+architected set; hygiene (ruff/vulture, R1) can go wider (a quarantined baselines/); layering (import-linter) covers the contract-having set (a viewer). A viewer/baseline package is legitimately in some gates, out of others.

mindscape`s three sets: arch (graph/astgrep/jscpd) = core neuroscan · hygiene (ruff/vulture) = core neuroscan baselines · layering (import-linter) = core neuroscan neuroviz.

FIX (small, fits the north star — mechanism identical, slot contents differ): keep `packages` as the PRIMARY list every gate defaults to; add optional per-gate override lists as computed vars / LOCAL-SLOTs in nox/ci/pre-commit, `{{ lint_packages | default(packages) }}` style. Most repos never set them; the 2-3 that differ do. Decide the minimal knob set — likely just a "hygiene adds" list (ruff/vulture wider) + confirm import-linter root_packages is already slottable via the import-contracts block (if not, slot it). Arch gates stay = packages (the strict owned set).

ACCEPTANCE: mindscape + cardioseg per-gate scopes reproducible from copier without hand-editing rendered gate files; a single-package repo (synthscape) is unchanged (all gates default to packages). e2e: a combo that sets a hygiene-extra package renders it into ruff/vulture only, not graph/astgrep.

Alternative if rejected: mindscape adopts with ci/nox/pyproject-deps as permanent un-synced local mods (register .copier-answers.yml + retrofit slot markers). Unblocks vip convergence either way. Origin: j08.
close_reason: Landed minimal-fork per-gate scope: vulture now config-driven ([tool.vulture] paths, vulture-scan slot; dropped redundant nox CLI *LAYERS so nox/CI/pre-commit agree); import-linter root_packages moved inside the import-contracts slot (layering scope repo-owned); arch (graph/astgrep/jscpd)+ruff stay =packages (abstract identical set, NOT slotted -> conform don't fork). coverage already slotted. No new questions, no new slots. A conforming repo (synthscape) renders identically. e2e locks it (test_multi_package). SPEC per-gate-scope section added. 66 passed.

title: Audit: mindscape adoption blocked by per-scan package granularity (v0.6 dry-run)
description:
Audit from attempting to adopt scaffold v0.6.0 into **mindscape** (which has NO `.copier-answers.yml` — its guardrails were hand-built in parallel, never templated). Generated v0.6 into a temp dir, diffed every template-owned file against mindscape's real ones. Question for the scaffold: is mindscape's divergence a REAL adoption blocker, or does the LOCAL-SLOT mechanism already cover it?

## Method
`copier copy --vcs-ref v0.6.0 -d project_name=mindscape -d packages="core,neuroscan,neuroviz" -d domain=ml -d coverage_floor=80` into scratch, then per-file diff.

## Same in both (the suite is genuinely shared)
7-axis gates all present: ruff · vulture · import-linter · ast-grep module-shape · graph.py fitness · jscpd · coverage floor · class-shape advisory (lcom/data_clumps/state_candidates). Same pinned versions (ruff 0.15.13, vulture 2.16). Detector logic near-identical — per-detector diffs are 6-10 lines = class-wrapping + a `LAYERS` var, NOT logic drift.

## What mindscape has that the template CANNOT express (the core tension)
1. **Per-scan package granularity.** mindscape scans DIFFERENT package sets per gate:
   - ruff + vulture: `core neuroscan baselines`
   - ast-grep + graph.py + jscpd: `core neuroscan` (deliberately EXCLUDES neuroviz web glue)
   - import-linter roots: `core neuroscan neuroviz`
   The template drives every gate from ONE flat `packages` list. No single answer to the `packages` question reproduces mindscape's layout. This is the sharpest gap.
2. **A 4th package (`baselines`)** that is in-scope for some gates, out for others.
3. **Divergent enforced ruff select.** mindscape enforces `W, E7, S101, T20` on top; v0.6 drops those and adds `SIM, T201`. (Overlaps sdlc-scaffold-vip.2 — the ruff-ratchet reconcile.)

## What mindscape keeps in the LOCAL-SLOTs (v0.6 already reserves these — NOT a blocker)
- coverage `omit` (40 hand-curated shells) → coverage-scan slot
- vulture `paths`/`exclude`/`ignore_names` (+forward, intra_op_num_threads, proj_out) → vulture-scan slot
- `[tool.structure]` thresholds → arch-thresholds slot
- import contracts (the two forbidden-import rules) → import-contracts slot
- ruff `extend-exclude` → ruff-exclude slot
These survive `copier update` by design. The slot mechanism DOES cover the config-value divergence.

## What mindscape has that lives OUTSIDE any slot (would be clobbered on overwrite)
- Full dep tree (~20 ML deps: torch/mne/moabb/mlflow/open-clip...) — template `[project.dependencies]` is not slotted
- `[tool.uv.index]` pytorch-cu130 wheel pin — not slotted
- `[tool.ruff.lint.per-file-ignores]` (tests get S101) — not slotted
- Rich README/CLAUDE/AGENTS portfolio content — template ships generic seeds

## What v0.6 gives mindscape that it lacks
- `.copier-answers.yml` registration (→ future `copier update`)
- LOCAL-SLOT markers (mindscape's pyproject has none → cannot receive updates cleanly today)
- `.gitattributes`, `docs/ROADMAP.md`, `devtools/README.md`
- Class-refactored detectors (obey py-top-level-function; mindscape's are older free-func style — cosmetic, devtools not ast-grep-scanned)
- `test_layout = "mirror"` knob (mindscape hardcodes mirror)
- Cleaner noxfile (`cov`/`gates` split, `venv_backend="none"`, explicit `success_codes`)
- pytest `--import-mode=importlib`

## The verdict-shaped question for the scaffold agent
mindscape is not BEHIND v0.6 — it's a customized SUPERSET. The config-value divergence is already handled by LOCAL-SLOTs. The genuinely-unexpressible part is **per-scan package granularity** (different pkg lists per gate) + the extra `baselines` package. 

Is that worth a template feature (e.g. per-gate package overrides, or slotted package lists in ci/nox/pre-commit), or is it a legit "this repo's shape is beyond the template, keep those files as permanent local mods" case? If the latter, adoption for mindscape = register `.copier-answers.yml` + retrofit slot markers + accept ci.yml/nox/pre-commit/pyproject-deps as un-synced local files. If the former, the template needs a `gate_packages` map before mindscape can adopt cleanly.
notes:
VERDICT (scaffold agent, 2026-07-14): mindscape is RIGHT, not wrong. Per-gate package scoping is a GENERAL house pattern — cardioseg does it too (vulture+import-linter include cardioview; coverage+ruff dont). The flat single packages list is the templates gap. NOT a hard blocker (config-value divergence already covered by LOCAL-SLOTs; mindscape can adopt now with 2-3 gate files as un-synced local mods). Real fix = per-gate package override slots defaulting to packages -> filed as a vip child. See that bead.
UNBLOCKED by vip.3 (v0.10.0). mindscape adopts with: packages=core,neuroscan (arch set); vulture paths widen to +baselines in the vulture-scan slot; import-linter root_packages +neuroviz in the import-contracts slot; coverage source in coverage-scan slot. Only non-slotted divergence = ruff-on-baselines -> conform (drop it, like cardioseg lints its arch set not baselines) rather than fork the template. Config-value divergence already covered by slots. mindscape can now register .copier-answers.yml + retrofit slot markers cleanly.
close_reason: Audit complete + gap fixed. VERDICT: mindscape is right, not wrong — per-gate scope is a general house pattern (cardioseg does it too); the flat packages list was the template gap, NOT a hard blocker (config-value divergence already slot-covered). FIX shipped: vip.3 (v0.10.0) — vulture config-driven, import-linter roots slotted, arch+ruff stay =packages (conform-dont-fork); no new questions/slots. Actual mindscape adoption tracked separately as mindscape-588n. Nothing left on the scaffold side.

title: Tier 2: ml-typing bundle — jaxtyping + beartype + shape_contracts (domain=ml, advisory)
description:
Parent: sdlc-scaffold-vip. Tier 2 = the ml-domain runtime-typing capability. 1/3 today (cardioseg only; mindscape/synthscape carry NEITHER), so it ships gated on domain==ml and ADVISORY — an opt-in-by-domain bundle, the ONE honest exception to the no-toggles rule (rji), justified because it is meaningless off a tensor codebase.

THE BUNDLE (pull from cardiac-seg):
- deps: jaxtyping + beartype (add to template pyproject deps, inside the {% if enable_ml %} block next to numpy). jaxtyping = Float[Tensor,"B C H W"] shape/dtype annotations (inert at runtime without a checker -> no prod cost); beartype = the runtime checker jaxtyping delegates to; cardioseg wraps them as @shapecheck in core.types, enforced in TESTS.
- ruff: ignore = ["F722"] — REQUIRED, jaxtyping shape strings ("b c h w") look like malformed forward refs to F722; jaxtypings own FAQ prescribes disabling it. Add F722 to the scaffolds ruff ignore, gated so it only appears for domain=ml (a non-ml repo has no jaxtyping strings, keep F722 enforced there). Decide: is the ignore list domain-conditional in the template, or F722 always-ignored (harmless if no jaxtyping)?
- shape_contracts.py — promote from cardiac-seg/devtools/ into template/devtools/ (MOVED here from the old 7g0 scope). --assert gate over tensor-shape BOUNDARIES (23 annotated in cardioseg); jaxtyping-style. domain==ml only. Engine+tests template-owned; portable params = jaxtyping type-name list; LOCAL-SLOT = array-aliases (each repos core.types alias names differ — Volume/Mask/... in cardioseg) via a [tool.shape_contracts] block or an inferred convention. Ship ADVISORY.
- The @shapecheck convention: decide whether the scaffold ships a starter core/types shapecheck helper + a docs note, or just the deps + gate and leaves the convention to the repo.

WIRING: advisory CI + nox step gated {% if enable_ml %}, like the class-shape gate #8. E2E: an ml-combo pos/neg fixture (an unannotated tensor boundary bites shape_contracts --assert); the non-ml combo must NOT ship any of it (assert jaxtyping/beartype/F722/shape_contracts absent).

OPEN (walk w/ owner): (a) advisory-forever vs ratchet-to-blocking for shape_contracts; (b) exact domain-gating seam for the ruff F722 ignore; (c) ship the @shapecheck helper or not; (d) array-alias parameterization (config list vs infer from core.types). Since its 1/3, this stays advisory until a 2nd repo adopts — then revisit promoting to a stronger default.

ROLLOUT: with the Tier-1 tag or its own. copier update cardiac-seg replaces its local shape_contracts.py; mindscape/synthscape get the bundle only if they set domain=ml AND opt in (both are ml domain but dont run typing yet — adoption is forward, not forced).

Adds SPEC gate #11 (shape_contracts, ml-only) + the ml-typing dep note. Origin: cardiac-seg-1ln7 / qqjs; jaxtyping bd zwno.
notes: RESCOPED by o70: shape_contracts is a BASE ml-gate (house bar for ALL domain=ml repos via enable_ml), NOT a cardiac-local extra. jaxtyping+beartype+F722-ignore = base ml deps/config. enable_ml already gates numpy/gitignore/doc-layers/data-CI, so a new {% if enable_ml %} gate needs NO new mechanism (union forbids permanent repo-local gates anyway). Advisory-first as a FIX runway, but it is the ml house bar, not opt-in-by-repo.
close_reason: Shipped + tested (green, 79 passed). ML-typing bundle landed enable_ml-gated: jaxtyping+beartype deps, F722 ignore, shape_contracts.py engine (generalized off core.obs->stdlib; array aliases via [tool.shape_contracts] FACT slot), advisory wiring in nox/CI/pre-commit, @shapecheck helper DOCUMENTED not shipped (r2w zero-package-code). Unit pos/neg + e2e ml-ships/non-ml-absent + --assert bite. Advisory-first (per-repo boundary migration is heavy); graduate-to-blocking is a later base flip once all ml repos clean.

title: EPIC: converge the 3 repos' guardrail architecture through the scaffold (staged adoption)
description:
Current reality (audited 2026-07-14, pyproject+devtools+deps across all 3): the three repos have DIVERGED on the guardrail rule-set, which violates the north star. cardioseg ratcheted furthest (added ~20 ruff codes: N/SIM/SLF001/PERF/C408-420/S603-607/PTH123/E741-3/PLR0124-1714/ICN001/E402, a pep8-naming block, jaxtyping+beartype runtime typing, and 2 new devtools engines magic_literals+shape_contracts). mindscape ratcheted a DIFFERENT direction (broad families C4/UP/SIM/PLR/T20/RUF; explicitly REJECTED N at 383 hits; has its own devtools/analytics.py). synthscape still sits on the scaffold`s EXACT narrow v0.6.0 list. scaffold v0.6.0 ships the narrow curated list.

GOAL: pull the generic/architecture deltas UP into the scaffold and roll them back out so the 3 reconverge — staged by EVIDENCE (majority of 3), gently, starting simple. Broaden adoption over time (get synth+mind onto each new scaffold rule, not just cardioseg). This epic tracks the convergence; children are the staged tiers.

STAGING (owner-endorsed "start simple, tiers 1+2 first, adopt more forward"):
- Tier 1 (child, start here): additive + majority/universal — magic_literals engine (portable, any repo) + add SIM to the scaffold superset (2/3: cardio+mind). See child.
- Tier 2 (child): ml-domain typing bundle — jaxtyping+beartype+shape_contracts+ruff ignore F722, gated on domain==ml, shipped ADVISORY (1/3 today, so opt-in-by-domain, the one honest exception to no-toggles). See child.
- Tier 3 (child): AMBIGUOUS placement — the rest of cardioseg`s ruff ratchet (N, SLF001, PERF, S603/607, PTH123, C4/UP, the pep8-naming mechanism + ML ignore-names slot). NOT dropped and NOT "permanent local" — parked pending 3-repo consensus on which become house-bar. Filed so the gap is tracked, not lost.

CROSS-CUTTING: as each rule graduates into the scaffold superset, the rollout = tag scaffold -> copier update cardiac-seg (origin, replace local copies) -> mindscape -> synthscape (sequence around synthscape-5vh v0.6.0 update). The measure of done for the epic: the generic gate config is copier-generated + identical across the 3; only LOCAL-SLOT facts differ.

AUDIT BOUNDARY (honest): diffed pyproject + devtools filenames + deps across the 3. NOT yet diffed: CI workflow YAML, the @shapecheck convention internals (core.types), the deferred `ty` type-checker axis (cardioseg bd rp8l), mindscape`s analytics.py engine. Those may surface more gaps -> add children as found.
design: NORTH STAR (owner): the GENERIC ARCHITECTURE is identical across cardiac-seg / mindscape / synthscape — engines (devtools/*.py), gate wiring (nox/CI/pre-commit), the RULE SET (ruff select, the gate inventory), and structure. There is NO permanent project-local ARCHITECTURE. The only per-project thing is genuine FACTS filling LOCAL-SLOTs: scan paths = `packages`, domain thresholds, pep8 ignore-names VOCAB (each repo`s physics/ML symbols), vulture domain FPs. Mechanism identical; only slot CONTENTS differ. EVERYTHING flows through the copier pipe — divergence is drift to be healed, not a steady state.
notes: REOPENED 2026-07-14: close was premature. Two real convergence blockers surfaced from consumer adoption (4c2 E501-enforced blocks cardiac 44o4; 8ex SLF001 union-vs-astgrep structural conflict = 99 hits on mind) + vip.4 shape graduation must be DONE not detached (owner). Finishing all three for real, then reverifying + closing.
close_reason:
CONVERGENCE ACHIEVED — closed on verified evidence, NOT deferred (owner: finish for real). Every delta the 3 repos had diverged on is now pulled UP into the scaffold and every convergence DECISION is resolved:

RULE SET (o70 union, byte-identical): union select defined + adopted; S101 IN (owner); E501 (cosmetic) + SLF001 (conflicts with the op-namespace ast-grep gate) demoted to the advisory surface house-wide (4c2/8ex) — NOT per-repo slots. Enforced union = 39 codes, identical across all 3.
ENGINES: all 8 devtools/*.py byte-identical to the template in cardiac/mind/synth (verified vs v0.16.0 tag).
GATES: magic_literals ENFORCED ratchet (2cj); shape_contracts GRADUATED advisory->blocking (vip.4, not detached); instability/main-sequence advisory (x3b); per-gate scope FACT slots (vip.3); hygiene-scope widen (9mu).
SUPERSET: scaffold expresses every consumer superset with zero loss (skr: F722 CLI, engine nargs, data_env_var + ci-step slots) + LICENSE (c64).
FACTS (the only per-repo variation, by design): packages, data_env_var, pep8 ignore-names vocab, magic ceilings, vulture FPs, shape aliases.

VERIFIED byte-level: ruff select SET == 41/39-code union in all 3; engines byte-identical; gate wiring all copier-generated from the one pinned template. Per the epic's DONE criterion (generic config copier-generated + identical across 3, only LOCAL-SLOT facts differ) — MET.

Children all done: 7g0, vip.1, vip.2, vip.3, vip.4 + 4c2, 8ex. Releases v0.14.0..v0.18.0, 92 tests green.

Consumer ADOPTION of the newest bar (v0.18) is the copier pipe doing its job, not a convergence gap: cardiac converged+clean (44o4 now unblocked by 4c2); mind converged (v0.13->v0.16 landed); synth converging live (us6). Divergence is HEALED — the 3 now share one copier-flowed guardrail architecture; future drift heals per tag via copier update. There is no scaffold-side work left and no rule dispute open.

title: Tier 1: promote magic_literals engine + add SIM to the scaffold ruff superset
description:
Parent: sdlc-scaffold-vip (convergence epic). Tier 1 = additive + majority/universal, the safe start.

SCOPE (shape_contracts + the jaxtyping/beartype bundle MOVED to the Tier 2 child — this bead is now magic_literals + SIM only):

1) magic_literals.py — promote from cardiac-seg/devtools/ into template/devtools/ (scaffold gate class "OURS, advisory", same as data_clumps/state_candidates/lcom). Owns the axis ruff PLR2004 cant: NON-comparison string literals + cross-file FREQUENCY + dict-schema key-sets (deduped vs ruff: ruff owns comparison operands, the detector excludes them). In cardioseg its a BLOCKING count-ratchet (--max-strings 48 --max-key-sets 11); in the scaffold ship ADVISORY first (a fresh gen has 0; each repo sets + ratchets its own floor in a LOCAL-SLOT config block).
   - Engine + unit tests = template-owned (tests/unit/test_devtools.py, equivalence-class). Ship verbatim (literal f-string braces -> NO .jinja).
   - Portable params = superset: token regex, default thresholds.
   - Project facts = LOCAL-SLOT / a [tool.magic_literals] block the engine reads: scan paths=packages, project stopwords, floor numbers.
   - Wiring: advisory CI + nox step like gate #8. E2E pos/neg fixture (a magic literal over floor bites).
   - domain gating: NONE — magic strings are universal, always-on (honors rji: no new enable_* toggle).

2) SIM (flake8-simplify) — add to the scaffolds ruff_select superset. Majority-justified: cardioseg AND mindscape both run it (2/3); synthscape doesnt yet. House-bar graduation, not a one-repo fit. The copier-update rollout brings synthscape onto it.

ROLLOUT: tag scaffold -> copier update cardiac-seg (origin; replace its local magic_literals.py copy) -> mindscape -> synthscape. First real template-OUTPUT change since v0.6.0 -> genuine consumer delta -> DO tag.

OPEN (walk w/ owner): magic_literals config surface ([tool.magic_literals] block vs CLI args vs copier answer); advisory-vs-ratchet default in the template (lean advisory, repo ratchets locally). SIM: any sub-code exclusions or whole-family.

Adds SPEC gate #10 (magic_literals). Origin: cardiac-seg-1ln7 / epic qqjs.
close_reason: Tier 1 landed: magic_literals.py promoted into template/devtools (stdlib logging, cardioseg refs stripped, detector logic verbatim, shipped ADVISORY - opt-in --max-* ratchet); wired advisory into nox/CI/pre-commit; SIM added to ruff_select superset (2/3 majority). Unit tests (pos/neg detector + ratchet bite) + e2e (ships-check, advisory-runs, end-to-end ratchet bite). 66 passed. SPEC gate #10 + README. Rollout: tag v0.7.0 -> copier update the 3 repos.

title: Drop ship_example: template ships zero code; e2e owns its fixture; handle empty cold-start
description:
Owner call (synthscape mq4 review): ship_example is another copier toggle that mostly muddies the mission. The scaffold claims guardrails-not-architecture, but ship_example=true ships a demo package (math_ops + pipeline) = shipping CODE. For the real use case (adoption into an existing repo — all 3 siblings) it is always false = dead weight. For a new project the demo is throwaway you delete in 30s.

The ONE thing it buys: a brand-new EMPTY repo generates red (coverage --fail-under=80 on 0%, graph.py --assert on empty packages). That cold-start crutch must be handled elsewhere before removing:

PLAN:
1. Move the demo to an E2E-ONLY fixture. The scaffold e2e currently relies on the shipped demo to have code to lint/test/graph; instead have the e2e generate a project THEN drop its own tiny module + test, then run gates. Template stops shipping any code.
2. Cold-start for a real new project: either (a) gates tolerate empty (coverage/test-mirror advisory until the first module lands), or (b) a one-line README new-project step: write your first module, then gates go green. Prefer (b) — do not weaken gates for the empty case.
3. Remove the ship_example toggle + package_name computed var + the {% if not ship_example %} _exclude entries. copier.yml shrinks.
4. MIGRATION: synthscape adopted at v0.4.2 with ship_example=false already, so its copier update to the toggle-less tag must resolve cleanly (drop the answer, no re-prompt). Test copier update on the synthscape checkout.

Pairs with sdlc-scaffold-rji (enable_* -> one multiselect). Both trim the copier surface toward: minimal prompts, template ships zero code.

title: copier.yml: collapse the enable_* bools into ONE multiselect (or profile), not 5 yes/no prompts
description:
Owner call (from synthscape mq4 review): the optional gates are 5+ independent bool prompts — enable_import_linter, enable_astgrep, enable_jscpd, enable_class_shape_smells, enable_beads (+ enforce_arch_fitness). Prompt sprawl at copy time + a combinatorial toggle-lattice the e2e must cover. Not really independent yes/no axes worth answering one-by-one.

PROPOSAL: one question instead. (A) multiselect list gates (copier multiselect, choices import_linter/astgrep/jscpd/class_shape/beads) — one prompt, answers file stores a list; or (B) profile enum minimal|standard|full mapping to fixed gate sets. Prefer A (granularity in one prompt) unless owner wants opinionated profiles.

TEMPLATE CHANGES: every if-enable_X in templates + copier.yml _exclude becomes an if-X-in-gates membership test. ci.yml / pyproject / devtools file-exclusion all key off it.

MIGRATION (must not break adopters): synthscape is adopted at v0.4.2 with the enable_* bool answers. A copier update to the new shape needs either (a) a copier _migrations hook mapping old bools -> the list, or (b) keep enable_* as computed when:false values derived from gates for back-compat, so old .copier-answers still resolve. Test copier update from v0.4.2 -> new tag on synthscape: NO re-answering, NO gate loss.

enforce_arch_fitness: decide if it joins the list or just becomes always-on (graph.py structural gate is near-core; likely drop its toggle entirely).

title: gitignore: anchor data/ -> /data/ (unanchored pattern hides a core/data/ package's tests)
description: template/.gitignore.jinja ships `data/` unanchored (meaning 'datasets out of repo'). But a project with a package path like core/data/ (synthscape) has tests at tests/unit/core/data/ — the bare `data/` matches THAT too, silently git-ignoring real test files. Found during synthscape mq4 adoption: tests/unit/core/data/test_synth.py was ignored -> CI graph.py --assert failed (missing mirror test), while local passed (file was on disk, just untracked). Fix: anchor to `/data/` (top-level only). Audit the rest of the gitignore for other unanchored dir patterns (runs/, node_modules/ are fine as those aren't source; data/ is the collision because it's a common package name).

title: Reduce folder-structure assumptions: test-mirror hard-codes tests/unit mirror layout
description:
AUDIT (from synthscape c3u adoption re-audit, owner flag: scaffold shouldn't assume folder structure beyond tests/). The scaffold claims 'guardrails, not architecture — imposes no layering' (README) but the test-mirror gate DOES impose a test-tree architecture: devtools/graph.py unmirrored() hard-defaults test_root='tests/unit' and requires tests/unit/<source-path>/test_<name>.py — i.e. the test tree MUST mirror the source tree. A project with a FLAT tests/unit/ (synthscape) is forced to restructure (~24 file moves) purely to satisfy layout, not correctness.

The imposition is not exposed anywhere adoptable: test_root is a def-arg, not a [tool.structure] slot or copier answer. So a project can't declare 'my tests are flat' or 'at tests/' without editing canonical gate code (which then conflicts on copier update).

DECIDE (one of):
1. Make test-mirror layout configurable — test_root + a mirror|flat mode in [tool.structure] LOCAL-SLOT (flat = 'a test_<name>.py exists ANYWHERE under tests/', mirror = current strict path). Lets flat-layout repos adopt without restructuring.
2. Keep strict-mirror but make it OPT-IN (an answer, default off) — mirror is a real discipline (one home per module) but not universal; don't force it on adoption.
3. Justify strict-mirror as a universal house rule and accept the restructure cost (then update the README 'no architecture' claim — it's not true, test layout IS imposed).

Lesser item: devtools/ is a fixed tool home (referenced in ci/nox/pre-commit/sgconfig). Probably acceptable (scaffold's own files) — note but likely no action. NON-issue: no research/ or src/ assumptions found; testpaths=tests is fine. Owner: keep optional toggles MINIMAL (pydantic stays baked, not a toggle) — so prefer option 2 or a single [tool.structure] key over a new copier answer if possible.

title: [external audit] post-refactor debt: stale LEARNINGS, nox≠CI parity, un-single-sourced hooks manifest
description:
Filed by an EXTERNAL agent — a Claude session running in the `interviews` project, read-only audit of sdlc-scaffold @ 8a95ed0. NOT the scaffold's own maintainer agent. Verify claims against the tree before acting; some are "verify", not "confirmed".

Refactor direction is good and the E2E was migrated correctly (ship_example=false adoption path + multi-package both tested). Debt below is the residue.

=== GAP 1 — docs/LEARNINGS.md is stale (highest-value, cheap) ===
25 viewer/import-linter references describing the PRE-refactor world (viewer 1→3 import-linter contracts, import-linter as a green core gate, core/ layer) — none of which ships now. SPEC.md was refreshed; LEARNINGS was not.
Worse: the "Migration path" recipe presets `viewer_imports_trainer=` / `has_viewer=` — flags that no longer exist in copier.yml. So the documented way to adopt the 3 real repos is broken against the current template.
Fix: rewrite LEARNINGS to the post-refactor contract; drop viewer/import-linter/core-layer; fix the migration presets to the current toggle set (packages, ship_example, enforce_arch_fitness, enable_astgrep/jscpd/class_shape, coverage_floor).

=== GAP 2 — local nox ≠ CI (leaks the headline claim) ===
noxfile.py.jinja `lint` is a WEAKER gate than ci.yml.jinja:
- CI runs advisory-full `ruff check . --statistics` + `ruff format --check .` over the whole tree AND vulture conf60 advisory. nox does format-check over LAYERS only, no advisory-full, no conf60. Dev passes nox, eats surprises in CI.
- VERIFY (possible harder bug): nox `lint` calls `graph.py --assert` (needs grimp/networkx from the `devtools` extra) but that session never `uv sync --extra devtools`; CI's tests-job does (ci.yml:74). If `uv run` doesn't auto-pull the optional extra, arch-fitness ERRORS under `nox lint` locally while passing in CI — the exact "green local / red CI" gap the scaffold exists to kill, inverted. E2E runs nox and passed, so uv may resolve it — confirm.

=== GAP 3 — .pre-commit-hooks.yaml breaks the single-source claim ===
copier single-sources ruff_version/vulture_version, but this ROOT manifest hardcodes `ruff@0.15.13` (line 5) / `vulture@2.16` (line 12) — not a .jinja, not rendered. Bump the copier var → silent drift. Also arg-less `graph.py --assert` (line 19) defaults to a package name that may not exist in a real project.
It is a stub ("UNUSED in the no-git MVP") — but it IS the remote-delivery path, so it bites the moment the network hop flips. Either render it from the single source, or add a test that asserts its versions == copier vars.

=== GAP 4 — remote/network hop unproven (biggest untested claim) ===
`repo: local → repo:<url> rev:vN` + `copier update` from a real REMOTE — all green locally/WSL, delivery stubbed (see GAP 3). Until a first push + a cross-repo update runs, "versioned central gates" is asserted, not shown. Not a code fix — a verification milestone.

=== MINOR ===
- gitignored sdlc_test/ scratch is a stale pre-refactor instance (still has sdlc_test_viz) — misleading if opened.
- {{project}}_DATA env (ci.yml:67) scaffolds a data-skip pattern the shipped example never exercises — aspirational, harmless.

(Deliberately NOT included: "not yet applied to a real repo" — normal/expected for this stage per the maintainer.)

It does NOT carry cardioseg's test-mirror rule — cardioseg/devtools/graph.py defines unmirrored() and does 'blocking += [test mirror ...] for m in unmirrored(packages)' (its graph.py line ~146, GRADUATED to blocking bd j4m2). Cardioseg exempts only __init__/__main__ (few shells); mindscape's omit carve generalizes better for projects with many shells. title: Instability / main-sequence coupling gate in graph.py (I = Ce/(Ce+Ca), distance D)
description:
Near-future. Sharpen the R3 coupling axis with Robert Martin`s package metrics — graph.py already computes the raw afferent/efferent counts, so this is additive.

WHAT graph.py HAS: Ca (afferent = fan-in), Ce (efferent = fan-out), betweenness, on the grimp import graph. Enforces direction (import-linter), acyclicity (no cycles), degree (god-module = Ca AND Ce both high).

WHAT TO ADD:
- Instability I = Ce / (Ce + Ca) per module/package (0 = maximally stable/depended-on, 1 = maximally unstable/depends-on-all). Trivial from the counts already gathered.
- (optional, needs an abstractness signal) Abstractness A = abstract-types / total-types; main-sequence distance D = |A + I - 1| — how far a package sits from the ideal stable<->abstract line (the "V"/stable-dependencies principle: arrows point toward stable abstractions).
- Report: a ranked I (and D if A is computed) table in the graph.py EXPLORER output (drop --assert), beside the existing fan-in/out/bottleneck/chokepoint tables.
- Gate: ADVISORY first (a number to watch), ratchet-to-blocking candidate — e.g. "a module moved further from the main sequence than its recorded baseline" (a per-module D ceiling in [tool.structure], same ratchet shape as file_max / magic-literals). Decide the exact assertion with owner before blocking.

WHY IT`S ITS OWN GATE, not god-module: god-module is a DEGREE check (both counts high). Instability is a RATIO/direction check — it catches a module that is unstable (high Ce) yet widely depended-on (high Ca would lower I, so specifically: depended-on things that are themselves volatile), the stable-dependencies violation a degree threshold misses. Complements, doesn`t duplicate.

OPEN: (a) abstractness signal for A/D — Python has no formal abstract/concrete split; approximate via ABC/Protocol/typing-only modules? or ship I only and defer A/D. (b) module vs package granularity. (c) advisory-forever vs D-ratchet; baseline capture. (d) does this belong in the scaffold superset for all repos or ml-only (it`s domain-neutral -> all repos).

Grounding: README "Coupling, in the standard vocabulary" section; Martin, Agile Software Development (SDP/SAP, the main sequence). Origin: owner insight while rewriting the README pyramid (R3 coupling axis). Relates to the convergence epic vip (a new house-bar gate -> rolls out through the pipe once landed).
close_reason: Done v0.16.0: Martin stability metrics in graph.py. instability(g) I=Ce/(Ce+Ca) (in_degree=Ca, out_degree=Ce; edges are importer->imported); abstractness(mod) A=abstract/total classes via AST (_is_abstract: ABC/Protocol base, metaclass=ABCMeta, or @abstractmethod); main_sequence_distance(g) D=|A+I-1|. Both in the explorer report tables. ADVISORY gate _off_main_sequence via [tool.structure] main_sequence_max, default 0.0 = OFF (a concrete stable leaf legitimately sits at D~1 -> no honest universal threshold, opt-in per repo; same honesty as file_min). Advisory-first = safe to add mid-rollout (never blocks; repos get it non-blocking). Unit: instability ratio, _is_abstract 3 forms, abstractness from file + None cases, distance + off-by-default. e2e: threshold ships. README + devtools README + SPEC updated.

title: Tier 3: reconcile the divergent ruff ratchet across the 3 repos (ambiguous placement)
description:
Parent: sdlc-scaffold-vip. Tier 3 = the gap that is REAL but has NO 3-repo consensus yet on whether/how it becomes house-bar. Filed so its tracked, NOT dropped and NOT declared permanent-local (per owner: nothing is permanently project-local; everything should flow through the pipe eventually — this is the "not yet decided where" bucket).

THE DIVERGENCE (audited 2026-07-14): the 3 repos ruff select ratcheted in DIFFERENT directions off the scaffolds narrow common floor:
- synthscape: scaffolds EXACT narrow list (no ratchet).
- mindscape: broad FAMILIES — C4, UP, T20, PLR, PLC, RUF, SIM, C90 (advisory target; enforced subset grows). Deliberately REJECTED N (383 hits).
- cardioseg: ~20 SPECIFIC codes — N (+ a [tool.ruff.lint.pep8-naming] ignore-names block), SLF001, PERF401, C408/C420, S603/S607, PTH123, E741/E742/E743, PLR0124, PLR1714, PLW3301, ICN001, E402, RUF005/007/010/012/022/046, PLW0108, E731.

WHY AMBIGUOUS: these are mostly 1/3 (cardioseg-only) or conflicting (mindscape broad-family vs cardioseg specific-code; N is ON in cardioseg, REJECTED in mindscape). By the scaffolds superset rule (majority of 3) NONE clears the bar today except SIM (already promoted in Tier 1). Promoting cardioseg`s taste wholesale = fitting the house bar to one repo — the exact anti-pattern the rule forbids.

THE WORK (a decision pass, not code-first):
1. Per-code triage across the 3: for each divergent code, is it (a) a bug-finder all 3 should run (-> promote to superset, roll out), (b) opinionated style that conflicts (-> pick ONE house answer WITH owner, or leave out), or (c) genuinely project-specific (-> a LOCAL-SLOT mechanism in the template, identical mechanism / different contents — e.g. the pep8-naming ignore-names is per-repo VOCAB but the block itself can be a shared slot).
2. Resolve the broad-family (mindscape) vs specific-code (cardioseg) STYLE clash — the scaffold must pick one expression of the select so generated config is identical. This is the core "generic architecture identical" tension.
3. Candidates likely to graduate as adoption widens (revisit each as a 2nd/3rd repo picks it up): SLF001, PERF401, the RUF safe-fix set, C408/C420. N + pep8-naming: decide the house line (cardioseg yes / mindscape no) — probably a domain=ml ML-idiom slot rather than a blanket N.
4. Output: an updated scaffold ruff_select (single source) + a pep8-naming LOCAL-SLOT mechanism, then the copier-update rollout so all 3 reconverge onto the SAME select (their only difference = LOCAL-SLOT vocab).

This is intentionally the slow tier — start Tier 1+2, widen adoption, come back and let evidence (2nd/3rd-repo uptake) decide each code. Origin: cardiac-seg ruff bd l79o / l5q8; mindscape + synthscape pyproject audits.
notes:
POST-ADOPTION UPDATE (mindscape adopted v0.10 on 2026-07-14, bd mindscape-588n): mindscapes line in the divergence above is now STALE — on adoption it took the scaffolds narrow curated select and thereby DROPPED its broad-family advisory + 3 enforced codes.
